content:
# Session 2 (iter-002) — review

## Metadata
- **No prover lane** this iter (`attempts_raw.jsonl` line-1 `no_prover_lane: true`).
- Sorry count: unchanged. GR-quot leg delivered + axiom-clean since iter-001; 26 open sorries are all deferred-by-directive or out-of-cone.
- `git diff HEAD~1`: n/a (subproject has a single commit `c53d9c9`); no `.lean` edits this iter.
- `sync_leanok`: iter=002, sha `c001f99`, added 0 / removed 0, chapters_touched [] → marker tree stable, no laundering risk.

## What happened
Plan agent surveyed all 26 remaining open sorries and dispatched **no prover** under the MECHANICAL hard gate (correct):
- **SNAP** (11, `SectionGradedRing.lean`) — DEFERRED by standing directive (sibling-import; don't diverge from `FBC-B_SNAP-chain`).
- **χ** (`QuotScheme.lean`: `hilbertPolynomial`, `QuotFunctor`, dependent `Grassmannian` functor) — DEFERRED, fills from cohomology leg at merge.
- **Out-of-cone**: weak `Grassmannian.representable` skeleton (tracked debt, goal doesn't need it); `RelativeSpec.lean`'s 7 "sorry" greps are all prose/docstrings, zero real proof-term sorry.

→ Zero closable in-leg sorries. Leg is at terminal "delivered, merge-pending" state. Dispatching deferred nodes would violate user standing directives + risk diverging the shared/merge encodings.

## Blueprint-doctor (logs/iter-002/blueprint-doctor.md)
Findings present but **all extraction artifacts deferred-to-merge** — confirmed via `extract-manifest.json` (mode `extract`, 3 GR seeds, `prefer: source`):
- `Cohomology_FlatBaseChange.tex`: 2 `% archon:covers` non-existent files + 14 broken `\ref` (dropped FBC leg).
- `Picard_GlueDescent.tex`: 4 broken `\cref`/`\uses` (2 labels).
- `Picard_QuotScheme.tex`: 13 broken `\cref` (localization/descent cluster).
- `Picard_SectionGradedRing.tex`: 7 broken `\cref`/`\uses`.

All target labels/files **outside the extracted 3-seed cone**; they resolve when the cone merges back into the parent's full chapter set. NOT edited — this leg's blueprint is byte-identical to parent+sibling; deleting refs / stubbing labels would corrupt the three-way merge (load-bearing merge-back discipline). Same disposition for the 326 leandag-unmatched parent-inherited helpers.

## Blueprint markers updated (manual)
- None. No prover work; `sync_leanok` made 0 changes; no `\mathlibok`/`\lean{}`/`% NOTE:` warranted; no stale `\notready` to strip in-cone.

## Notes
- No new 1-to-1 coverage debt introduced (no Lean decls added). The unmatched backlog is pre-existing extraction inheritance.

## Recommendation
See `recommendations.md` — in short: do NOT re-dispatch on deferred/out-of-cone sorries; leg is merge-pending. Next substantive action is external (merge-back, or a USER_HINTS redirect).
